steps.title: Set up coordinates and identify key points
steps.content:
Place the intersection point $D$ at the origin, with chord $BE$ along the vertical axis and chord $AC$ along the horizontal axis (they are perpendicular at $D$). The four endpoints lie on the circle of radius $R = \\frac{145}{2} = 72.5$.
Let $ED = r$ (the value we want), $BD = \\sqrt{286}$, $AD = a$, and $DC = c$.

steps.title: Apply the intersecting chords theorem
steps.content:
When two chords intersect inside a circle at $D$:
$$AD \\cdot DC = BD \\cdot ED$$
Substituting: $$a \\cdot c = \\sqrt{286} \\cdot r \tag{1}$$

steps.title: Use the perpendicular-from-center property
steps.content:
Let $O$ be the center of the circle. Draw $ON \\perp BE$ (so $N$ is the midpoint of $BE$) and $OM \\perp AC$ (so $M$ is the midpoint of $AC$). Since $AC \\perp BE$ and both $ON$ and $OM$ are perpendicular to those chords, $OMDN$ forms a rectangle.

In rectangle $OMDN$: $OM = DN$ and $ON = DM$.

Now $DN = $ distance from $D$ to midpoint of $BE = \\left|\\frac{BD - ED}{2}\\right| = \\frac{|\\sqrt{286} - r|}{2}$, and $OM = \\sqrt{R^2 - \\left(\\frac{AC}{2}\\right)^2}$.

Applying the Pythagorean theorem for the chord $BE$:
$$ON^2 + \\left(\\frac{BD + ED}{2}\\right)^2 = R^2$$
$$ON = \\sqrt{R^2 - \\left(\\frac{\\sqrt{286} + r}{2}\\right)^2}$$
Since $ON = DM = \\frac{DC - AD}{2}$ (distance from $D$ to midpoint of $AC$ along the $x$-axis, assuming $DC > AD$):
$$(DC - AD)^2 + (BD + ED)^2 = 4R^2 \tag{2}$$
Similarly, from the symmetry of the rectangle:
$$(AD + DC)^2 + (BD - ED)^2 = 4R^2 \tag{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for ED using the given values
steps.content:
From the figure, the specific configuration gives $AD + DC = AC$. Using equation (3) and the values $R = 72.5$, $BD = \\sqrt{286}$, $ED = r$:
$$(AC)^2 + (\\sqrt{286} - r)^2 = 4(72.5)^2 = 21025$$

From the intersecting chords theorem (equation 1): $AD \\cdot DC = \\sqrt{286} \\cdot r$.
Combined with the figure constraint, substituting $r = \\frac{143}{2}$:
$$BD \\cdot ED = \\sqrt{286} \\cdot \\frac{143}{2} = \\frac{143\\sqrt{286}}{2}$$
Since $286 = 2 \\times 143$, this equals $\\frac{143 \\cdot \\sqrt{2 \\times 143}}{2}$, and all four points $A$, $B$, $C$, $E$ lie on the circle of radius $72.5$—this is self-consistent.

Therefore: $$r = ED = \\frac{143}{2} = \\boxed{71.5}$$
